title: Rich Fluffy Slovakian Pork Dumplings
description: A hearty Slovakian dish featuring fluffy pork dumplings with a hint of mustard and whole black peppercorns, perfect for a crowd. This halal-friendly recipe is a crowd-pleaser.
instructions: Combine ground pork, flour, eggs, whole black peppercorns, mustard, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Mix the ingredients together with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Gradually add the chicken broth to the mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into small pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape using a rolling pin or your hands.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the dumplings.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook the dumplings for about 10 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the water with a slotted spoon and serve with melted butter.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
